text.text:
We were recommended this spot and it absolutely delivered. The standout dish for us was the Wife’s Special Noodles, especially when mixed with the braised beef. The flavors were deep, comforting, and well balanced, and the beef added richness that really elevated the dish. Easily our favorite of the meal.
We also ordered the Persian cucumber appetizer, which was crisp, refreshing, and packed with flavor. The dumplings were excellent as well. Both the pork and shrimp and pork and leek dumplings were very juicy, with wrappers that were just right, not too thick or too thin.
The atmosphere is simple and casual. Nothing fancy, but clean and comfortable. Service was a tier above what you typically expect at a neighborhood Chinese restaurant. Attentive, efficient, and friendly without being overbearing.
Food: 4.5/5
Service: 4.5/5
Ambiance: 4/5
Overall, a strong recommendation and a place I will definitely be coming back to when I’m craving noodles or dumplings.

text.text:
Lao Xi Noodle House is one of those spots that doesn’t try too hard but ends up being amazing because of it. If you’re looking for authentic, comforting noodle dishes, this place delivers every time. Their cucumber salad is one of my favourites and a must, but the noodles are the star. You can really tell they’re freshly made. Perfect texture and they hold onto the sauce and broth so well. The beef noodle soup is especially good, with a rich, flavourful broth that tastes like it’s been simmering all day.
The portions are generous, and the prices are very reasonable for the quality you’re getting.
Overall, it’s a go-to spot for authentic noodles. Nothing fancy, just really good food

text.text:
Been on my list for a while and finally came in on a Saturday for lunch. The noodles were to die for! I got the special with three toppings and it was so perfect and delicious. The meat sauce and tomato egg brought back my childhood memories the nostalgia! The beef cubes were so tender and flavorful. The noodles are perfectly al dente with a little bite. Not to mention the bowl is huge! This place is legit!

The dumplings were good as well but I prefer some of the other SGV greats.

Service is quick and friendly. No wait when I went around 1pm. This is now my go to when I get noodle cravings!

text.text:
Wonderful atmosphere, great food! Went here after a very long walk at The Arboretum in Arcadia. Nourishing eats that didn’t disappoint. My friend had the lamb soup with cat and glass noodle, I had the cold sesame noodles. My noodles were amazing, I took them spicy, right amount of heat. My friends lamb soup had black fungi, potatoes, glass noodles, cat ear noodles, and lamb. I’m not a lamb fan but I tried some and it was very flavorful. My friend’s lamb soup raved on and on about it. Brought home leftovers of her soup and my sauce. Did someone say seconds? I’m from Pennsylvania, but if I lived around here I’d be back again soon! Thank you 🙏
Oh and the spring onion pancakes were delicious!!!

text.text:
A place that inspires nostalgia. I saw a couple of kids in there doing homework and people in the area going there for some lunch or early dinner. The kids art on the wall was so sweet and it really made the place feel like home. The soup was so fragrant and I could easily change my noodles for something I wanted. I love this style of noodle, I got the knife cut and it was so chewy, and the beef was so tender I could pull it apart with my chopsticks. I recommend this spot if you’re in the area. Because it was a lot slower I was sitting at the booth with an empty bowl for a minute, but since I wasn’t in a rush I didn’t mind at all.
I recommend the b7 (or d7 I can’t remember) in their house specialties. The broth was delicious!
